objective: Harden data-extension preview and relationship governance without schema changes
rollbackNotes: Preserve unrelated user changes; revert only this checkpoint scope if explicitly approved.
nextAction: Resume from additive Contact Builder governance: first-class provenance/classification/audit tables, import/source metadata, and frontend relationship designer remain follow-up work.
outcome: Completed a no-schema audience-service governance slice for Contact Builder data-designer safety. Data-extension DTOs now cascade nested validation and cap key request collections; relationship definitions require cardinality. DataExtensionService now fails closed on unsupported preview relationship paths and unknown sort fields, sorts query-preview rows before projection, enforces relationship cardinality/type/primary-key rules, requires sendable fields to be required, requires configured primary keys to be required and marked primary, clears sendable metadata when disabled, and blocks sendable-key changes once records exist. SegmentService now rejects data-extension/relationship-style segment rule metadata instead of treating it as subscriber custom fields.
validationRun: PASS: git diff --check on services/audience-service and checkpoint/audit files, with CRLF warnings only
residualRisks: No first-class data-extension provenance, classification, or governance-audit tables were added in this no-schema slice.
Relationship metadata remains JSON; relationship joins/query execution remain intentionally unsupported until indexed schema and performance design exist.
Existing record-level sendable-key uniqueness/null checks are not proven for historical rows; immutable changes now require a reviewed migration path.
Frontend data-extension designer still needs typed governance UI after backend contracts are expanded.
